title: excoecaria chamelaea
intro: Excoecaria chamelaea, also known as the Yellow Oleander or Yellow Sap, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant's extracts have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against cell damage. Excoecaria chamelaea is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to be effective in alleviating various health issues.
